violations.text: The reach-in cooler labeled "Fridge 2" was observed in poor repair (maintaining ambient temperature of 49 degrees Fahrenheit). The unit is to not be used until it can be repaired/replaced/serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2026

Milk was observed held at 43 degrees Fahrenheit. The reach-in refrigerator was observe at 48 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Milk was moved to a different refrigerator to rapidly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2025

The milk reach-in refrigerator was observed in poor repair (maintaining ambient temperature of 48 degrees Fahrenheit). The unit is to not be used until it can be repaired/replaced/serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2025
No person in charge certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH Center for food protection at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2024

Food was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Food was removed at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2024

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 150-400 ppm. New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2024

Packaged chicken fajitas was observed held at (120) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling PIC reheated fajitas to 165 degrees for fifteen seconds and returned to hot hold unit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2023
